notes.anchor: the thirty days fixed --- by the Cornelian law
notes.note: Under Sulla's lex Cornelia de provinciis ordinandis (81 BC), a governor had thirty days to quit his province once his successor had arrived; Cicero notes that Appius withdrew so far that meeting within the term was impossible.

Latin forum agere: a governor's exercise of jurisdiction at the assize (conventus), hearing cases and issuing decrees; Cicero implies it was unseemly for an outgoing governor to keep doing so once succeeded.
Praefectus evocatorum: the officer commanding the evocati, time-served veterans recalled to service who formed a seasoned corps and were exempt from ordinary duties.
A cohort was a tenth of a legion (nominally about 480 men); Cicero complains that three of his already scant cohorts had gone missing under Appius's command.
